textPlain: I know a couple of people who work these kinds of shifts, and a major headache is trading shifts so that people can attend life events. If you could program that into this you could have a pretty interesting product.

That is more the area I f workforce management systems, and they are truly big business.I’ve previously tried starting a scheduling company, and even when one has a product that in testing shows that it would save the potential customers lots of money, it is really hard to gain traction.
Younger me would jump on that problem. The issue, of course, is that by the time you're making allowances for life events, trading favors, etc. the problem isn't technical anymore.
